description_long: This object is a heavy-duty axe featuring a robust, rectangular steel head with a flat poll and a broad, wedge-shaped cutting edge. The head is painted or coated in a dark, nearly black finish, giving it a utilitarian and durable appearance. The handle is made of light-colored wood, likely hickory or ash, and is smoothly contoured with a gentle curve for ergonomic grip and efficient force transfer. The handle is securely fitted into the eye of the axe head, with a visible wedge or pin at the top to ensure a tight fit. The overall design is practical and functional, suitable for splitting wood or demolition tasks in urban environments such as parks, garages, or construction sites. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, emphasizing its straightforward, work-oriented purpose.
description: A heavy-duty axe with a dark, rectangular steel head and a smoothly curved light wood handle, designed for practical use. The utilitarian design features a broad cutting edge and a secure, wedged handle fit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, flat face of the axe head and the full width of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the length of the handle and the wedge-shaped blade edge of the axe head.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the poll (back) of the axe head and the handle's attachment point.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the handle's curvature and the thickness of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, smooth finish, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular steel head with a single broad wedge-shaped edge and flat poll, no beard, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible wedge at the top of the handle.
